steps.title: Use the trend to fix the slope's sign
steps.content: The scatterplot's $y$-values rise as $x$ rises, from about $(1, 25)$ at the left to $(5, 65)$ at the right. The slope is $$\frac{65 - 25}{5 - 1} = 10,$$ which is positive. Choices A and C both have slope $-10$, so they cannot model increasing data.

steps.title: Pick between the remaining choices with one data point
steps.content:
Choices B and D both have slope $10$ but opposite signs on the intercept ($+14.7$ vs $-14.7$). Test at $(1, 25)$:
• Choice B: $y = 14.7 + 10(1) = 24.7$ — matches the data point
• Choice D: $y = -14.7 + 10(1) = -4.7$ — nowhere near the data
That matches choice B — B is correct.
